description: Move WebSocket connection lifecycle from Chat component to an app-level WebSocket context, enabling eager connection right after authentication.
steps: Step 1: Update `frontend/src/services/api.ts` - Make sessionId optional in `getPresignedWebSocketUrl()`. Change the signature to `getPresignedWebSocketUrl(accessToken: string, sessionId?: string)` (note: reorder params so accessToken comes first since it's always required). Update the fetch body to only include sessionId if provided: `body: JSON.stringify(sessionId ? { sessionId } : {})`.

Step 2: Update `frontend/src/services/websocket.ts` - Make sessionId optional in the `connect()` method signature: `async connect(sessionId?: string): Promise<void>`. Update the call to `apiService.getPresignedWebSocketUrl(accessToken, sessionId)` to match the new API signature. The rest of the WebSocket connection logic remains the same.

Step 3: Create `frontend/src/contexts/WebSocketContext.tsx` - Create a new context following the AuthContext pattern. The context should: (a) Export a `WebSocketProvider` component that takes children. (b) Use `useAuth()` to watch for authenticated user. (c) When user becomes authenticated (user !== null and !isLoading), immediately call `wsService.connect()` without a sessionId. (d) Expose via context: `wsService` (the WebSocketService instance), `connectionStatus` ('disconnected' | 'connecting' | 'connected'), `sendMessage(text: string, sessionId: string, userId?: string)` helper. (e) Handle reconnection logic (visibility change, online event) - move this from Chat.tsx. (f) Handle disconnect on logout (when user becomes null). (g) Export a `useWebSocket()` hook similar to `useAuth()`. Important: Create a NEW WebSocketService instance inside the provider (don't use the exported singleton) so the lifecycle is properly managed.

Step 4: Update `frontend/src/App.tsx` - Import `WebSocketProvider` from `./contexts/WebSocketContext`. Wrap the Routes inside `<WebSocketProvider>` which itself is inside `<AuthProvider>`. The tree becomes: AuthProvider > WebSocketProvider > Routes.

Step 5: Refactor `frontend/src/components/Chat.tsx` - Remove all WebSocket lifecycle code (connectWebSocket function, wsServiceRef, connectionStatus state, the useEffect that calls connectWebSocket, the useEffect for visibility/online reconnection). Instead: (a) Import and use `useWebSocket()` hook to get `connectionStatus`, `wsService`, `sendMessage`. (b) Keep all the stream event handlers (handleStreamEvent, handleComplete, handleError, handleClose) but subscribe/unsubscribe them via wsService.on/off in a useEffect. (c) The `sendMessage` local function should use the context's sendMessage or directly call `wsService.sendQuery(text, sessionId, userId)`. (d) Keep the session ID generation logic, message UI state, streaming state, etc. (e) Keep the auto-send initial query logic but trigger it based on connectionStatus from context.
acceptance_criteria: TypeScript compiles without errors: `npx tsc --noEmit` passes in frontend/
Vite build succeeds: `npm run build` passes in frontend/
WebSocketContext.tsx exists and exports WebSocketProvider and useWebSocket hook
App.tsx wraps routes with WebSocketProvider inside AuthProvider
Chat.tsx no longer imports WebSocketService directly or manages connection lifecycle
Chat.tsx uses useWebSocket() hook for connection status and sending messages
The api.ts getPresignedWebSocketUrl method accepts sessionId as optional
The websocket.ts connect() method accepts sessionId as optional
Reconnection logic (visibility change, online event) lives in WebSocketContext, not Chat
verification: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` in frontend/ directory and confirm zero errors
Run `npm run build` in frontend/ directory and confirm successful build output
findings: TypeScript strict mode with exactOptionalPropertyTypes had no issues since the optional sessionId param uses standard '?' syntax. NODE_OPTIONS env var needed to be cleared to run tsc/build due to a missing proxy-bootstrap.js preload module. Build produces clean output with no warnings.
